TSLA: Tesla Has Abandoned the Model S and Mercedes Wants to Fill the High-End EV Void. Tesla has discontinued its Model S, creating a void in the high-end electric vehicle market. A rival automaker now sees this as an opportunity to introduce its own luxury EV to fill the gap left by Tesla's decision.

Cullen Frost Bankers Inc. Acquires 5,813 Shares of Tesla, Inc. $TSLA. Cullen Frost Bankers Inc. increased its stake in Tesla by 9.1% in the fourth quarter, acquiring an additional 5,813 shares to hold 69,513 shares valued at approximately $31.3 million. This purchase contributes to Tesla's significant institutional ownership, which stands at 66.20%. Analyst sentiment for Tesla is mixed, with a "Hold" consensus rating and an average price target of $395.20, despite recent price target increases and "Buy" ratings from some firms.

Edison International Teams Up with Tesla for Battery Storage Projects. Edison International has partnered with Tesla to launch new Tesla Energy products, focusing on battery storage projects. Southern California Edison (SCE) and SoCore Energy are implementing systems featuring Tesla Powerpacks for both residential and commercial customers. These initiatives aim to expand the use of clean renewable power and reduce battery storage system costs through demand response demonstration projects.

YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.2123. The YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (TSLY) has declared a weekly distribution of $0.2123 per share. This announcement highlights the ETF's strategy to provide income through options, with the distribution record date set for June 3, 2026, and the payable date for June 5, 2026. Shareholders should note that distributions are subject to change and are not guaranteed.

Two Tesla Robotaxi Crashes In Austin Are Putting Fresh Pressure On Elon Musk's EV Giant As Youtuber Says It's Time To 'Come Clean'. YouTuber Zack Nelson has called on Elon Musk to address recent reports of Tesla Robotaxi crashes in Austin. TechCrunch revealed unredacted NHTSA documents showing two low-speed incidents since July 2025 involving supervised robotaxis, where a teleoperator intervention led to a crash with a metal fence. This news adds to previous reports of 5 additional Robotaxi crashes in Austin in January, totaling over 14 incidents since mid-2025, amidst ongoing criticism of Tesla's autonomous driving technology compared to competitors like Waymo.

TD Bank (TD) offers autocallable notes with 6.90% contingent interest. TD Bank is offering Autocallable Contingent Interest Notes linked to the performance of Meta Platforms (META), NVIDIA (NVDA), and Tesla (TSLA) stocks. These notes provide a 6.90% contingent annual interest rate, with payments dependent on each reference asset meeting monthly performance thresholds. The notes, which have a principal amount of $1,000 and mature on May 20, 2031, also feature monthly call observation dates where they may be automatically redeemed.

Barclays (DJP) launches Buffered Autocallable Notes linked to TSLA with 20% buffer. Barclays Bank PLC is offering Buffered Autocallable Notes linked to Tesla (TSLA) common stock, featuring a 20.00% buffer against losses. These notes have a principal amount of $1,000, an Issue Date of June 2, 2026, and a Maturity Date of May 31, 2030, with a periodic Call Premium of $170 (17.00% per annum) if automatically redeemed. Investors are exposed to Tesla's stock performance with capped upside and significant downside risk if the buffer is breached, making them suitable for investors willing to accept limited upside for partial downside protection and issuer credit risk.

TD Bank (TD) 15.05% auto‑callable notes linked to AMD/META/MU/TSLA. TD Bank is offering Series H equity-linked, auto-callable notes with a 15.05% annual coupon, maturing in May 2029. These notes are linked to the performance of AMD, META, MU, and TSLA, with payments dependent on the lowest performing stock. The notes feature monthly callable observation dates and a 20% buffered downside protection, exposing investors to potential losses if the lowest performing underlying stock falls significantly below its starting price.

REX TSLA Growth & Income ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.2013. REX TSLA Growth & Income ETF announced a weekly distribution of $0.2013 per share. This distribution provides investors with regular income from the ETF. The announcement highlights the ETF's income-generating aspect related to TSLA.
TGT: Target: Fiscal Q1 Earnings Snapshot. Target Corporation (TGT) has reported fiscal first-quarter profits of $781 million, translating to $1.71 per share, which surpassed Wall Street's expectations of $1.41 per share. The retailer's revenue for the quarter was $25.44 billion, also beating analyst forecasts. Target anticipates full-year earnings to be between $7.50 and $8.50 per share.

Target Corp. stock (US87612E1064): Q1 2026 earnings call puts US retailer in the spotlight. Target Corp. is set to hold its Q1 2026 earnings call on May 20, 2026, where investors will look for insights into consumer demand, margins, and strategy in the competitive US retail environment. The company's business model leverages large-format stores, owned brands, and integrated digital services, with a focus on blending physical and digital experiences for customers. Key areas of interest for investors include commentary on comparable store sales, traffic, ticket size, digital growth, inventory management, and cost dynamics.

Target Corp. stock (US87612E1064): new supply chain chief and 2026 share price momentum. Target Corp. has appointed Jeff England, a former Walmart executive, as its new chief of global supply chain and logistics to address inventory and distribution challenges. The company's stock has seen a 25% year-to-date increase in 2026, trading near a mean analyst price target of $128, and reflects renewed optimism despite past volatility. This strategic leadership change is expected to improve operational efficiency and is significant for investors observing the domestic retail sector and consumer spending trends.

QXO exec leaves for Target. Jeff England, formerly QXO's chief supply chain officer, has been hired by Target Corp. to lead its global supply chain and logistics efforts, starting May 31. England's career includes experience at Genuine Parts Company and Walmart, and he is credited with improving inventory availability and reducing transportation costs at QXO. This move comes as QXO, led by Brad Jacobs, is rapidly expanding in the building products space with ambitious sales targets.

Target Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day. Target Corp. (TGT) stock increased by 3.11% to $127.24, outperforming the broader market during a negative trading session where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both fell. This marks the second consecutive day of gains for Target's stock.
SBUX: HighTower Advisors LLC Purchases 350,364 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. HighTower Advisors LLC increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 24.2% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 350,364 shares and bringing their total holdings to 1,795,943 shares valued at $151,236,000. Other hedge funds also adjusted their positions in Starbucks, with institutional investors owning 72.29% of the company's stock. Starbucks recently released strong earnings results, reporting $0.50 EPS which beat analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.62 per share.

Starbucks stock (US8552441094): investors react to Q2 results and leadership news. Starbucks recently reported disappointing fiscal second-quarter results and cut its full-year guidance, leading to a sharp sell-off in its stock. The company's core business relies on company-operated and licensed stores, digital engagement, and brand partnerships, but faces challenges from macroeconomic conditions and competition, particularly in China. Investors are closely monitoring its earnings, capital allocation, and regional growth as these factors influence its future performance and market valuation.

Starbucks Corp stock (US8552441094): dividend yield, price swings and what’s next for the coffee giant. This article examines Starbucks Corp (SBUX) stock, focusing on its dividend yield, recent price volatility, and future outlook amidst changing consumer demand. It details the company's core business model, key revenue drivers, and competitive position in the global coffee market, highlighting its brand strength and digital strategies. The piece also discusses Starbucks' significance for US investors, linking its performance to consumer health and international market exposure, while emphasizing a balance of risks and rewards for potential investors.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS) Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts. The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS) has garnered a "Moderate Buy" consensus recommendation from 23 research firms, with an average 12-month price target of $134.47. The company recently exceeded quarterly earnings expectations with an EPS of $1.57 and revenue of $25.17 billion, a 6.5% year-over-year increase. Despite a new lawsuit concerning facial recognition, analysts have lifted some earnings estimates, and Disney has launched a "Blockbuster Summer" campaign to boost brand momentum.

Disney and Epic Games Bring ‘Star Wars’ Stories to Fortnite Fans. Disney and Epic Games are partnering to integrate 'Star Wars' content into Fortnite, coinciding with the theatrical release of 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu'. This collaboration includes a dedicated "Watch Party Island" in Fortnite featuring a sneak peek of the film and developer Q&A, and the release of a Star Wars Toolkit for creators. The initiative extends to Disney Parks attractions, aiming to provide interactive and immersive fan experiences across various platforms.

Hulu on Disney+ Just Got Better: What You Need to Know About Linking Your Profiles. Disney+ is enhancing the integration of Hulu content by allowing eligible bundle subscribers to link their Hulu profiles, bringing watch history, watchlists, and recommendations directly into Disney+. This change ensures a seamless viewing experience, with Hulu content appearing in personalized sections and a dedicated Hulu Hub on Disney+. Upcoming features also include a new Live Guide for Disney+ users.
